@graph.description: RO-Crate package containing a sealed RunTrace, optional PROV export, routing decision, runtime context, and CAPAS physical evidence metadata.
@graph.name: CAPAS traced scientific computation crate

@graph.description: Draft profile extending Workflow Run RO-Crate-style traces with first-class physical evidence, witness independence, reference truth, and explicit success/no-evidence/failure/rejection states.
@graph.name: CAPAS Physical Evidence Profile

@graph.capas:decisionReason: explicit external engine workload; route is opt-in and engine provenance is recorded at execution
@graph.name: CAPAS costurero traced run

@graph.description: Workflow plan that ingests a scientific workload, records runtime context, estimates routing cost, executes or skips the selected engine, and attaches CAPAS physical-evidence metadata.
@graph.name: CAPAS costurero workflow

@graph.description: Structured scientific workload routed by the CAPAS costurero.
@graph.name: Scientific workload

@graph.description: Structured result summary, skipped state, or failure state.
@graph.name: Result summary or skipped/failure state

@graph.capas:reason: explicit external engine workload; route is opt-in and engine provenance is recorded at execution
@graph.name: Routing decision

capas:summary.claim_scope: randomized adversarial seed only; shows the scaling-law anchor is not a single hand-picked example, but it still is not an agent-generated corpus
capas:summary.expected: all randomized variants violate z=1 by more than preregistered tolerance
capas:summary.finite_size_notes: Deterministic randomized adversarial family generated with seed 20260617. Each variant samples an exponent in [0.35, 0.72], then applies small jitter while preserving positive decreasing gaps. The preregistered criterion requires all variants to pass local monotonicity and fail the z=1 +/-0.10 universal anchor.
capas:summary.observable: Randomized generated Ising finite-size gap exponents with wrong scaling
capas:summary.physical_evidence_detail: Randomized adversarial scaling family: all generated finite-size gap sequences are positive, finite, and decreasing, but every fitted exponent falls outside the preregistered z=1 +/-0.10 tolerance.
capas:summary.pre_registered_success_criterion: variant_count is 8, every variant has local_property_tests_pass true, and every variant has abs(fitted_exponent - 1.0) > 0.10

structure_mapping.generator_output: randomized family of finite-size gap sequences
structure_mapping.local_oracle_relation: each variant has positive finite gaps decreasing with system size
structure_mapping.preserved_relation: each generated sequence is judged by local monotonicity and universal scaling exponent
